kicker: PT LEVEL I // RUNNING THE LINE

beats.narration: The part remembers everything you do to it. The indication you read at the end is the sum of every step behind it.

payload.title: RUN THE LINE
payload.sub: Seven stations, one part, one honest result.

beats.narration: Seven stations, one chain. Pre-clean, prove the system, penetrant and dwell, removal, develop, inspect, postclean. Not seven skills — one continuous line.

payload.heading: The seven-station line
payload.note: PRE-CLEAN → PROVE SYSTEM → DWELL → REMOVE → DEVELOP → INSPECT → PROTECT

beats.narration: Two habits define a clean run. Verify before you process — light, system check, temperatures, proven good first. And trace every problem backward to its station.

payload.heading: The two habits that run the line

tiles.s: Prove the system before parts move
Every bad result names a station

beats.narration: Removal is where tests are won or lost. Stop at an acceptable background, not at zero — chase a flawless part and the evidence washes away.

payload.heading: Read the symptom, fix the station

beats.narration: So run all seven honestly and the method is bulletproof. Shortcut any one, and the line keeps moving while quietly finding nothing.

payload.sub: Shortcut one, and the line finds nothing.
